# Graphyn Code (@graphyn/code)

## TL;DR
`@graphyn/code` is Graphyn's developer CLI for agent-assisted software delivery.
It coordinates planning and execution across specialized agents so engineering work moves from request to shipped change faster.

## Who Needs This
- Developers who want execution support, not just chat output.
- Teams standardizing AI-assisted delivery workflows.
- Technical leads orchestrating architecture, backend, frontend, and review lanes.
- Agents integrating with Graphyn's CLI execution contract.

## What This Surface Delivers
- `graphyn` CLI entrypoints and interaction flows.
- Mission-control style orchestration and streaming interaction.
- Coordination modules for multi-agent execution across codebases.

## Why This Matters For Graphyn
Graphyn is built around command-native execution.
This repo is where that execution model becomes a daily engineering workflow.

## Runtime Truth Boundaries
- Reliability claims require build/test receipts.
- Orchestration and streaming error paths are production-critical and must stay explicit.

## Quickstart
```bash
npm install
npm run build
npm run dev
npm run test:package
```

## Public Links
- Product: https://graphyn.xyz/code
- Package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@graphyn/code

## Technical References
- Commander: https://github.com/tj/commander.js#readme
- Inquirer: https://github.com/SBoudrias/Inquirer.js#readme
- Chalk: https://github.com/chalk/chalk#readme
- Ora: https://github.com/sindresorhus/ora#readme
- Node.js APIs: https://nodejs.org/docs/latest-v22.x/api/

## For Agents
1. Preserve CLI contract and subcommand stability.
2. Keep orchestration failures explicit and observable.
3. Avoid regressions in automation-critical flows.
4. Provide build/test command receipts before closure claims.
